parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff adds 6600+ lines across critical runtime paths (retry, error dispatch, background job execution, API client) but leaves the core API client (glm-client.mjs, 155 lines changed) and job lifecycle modules (tracked-jobs.mjs, job-control.mjs) without dedicated test files. UAT evidence exists only for v0.4.4 and v0.4.5; the v0.4.6 features (BigModel error dispatch, auto retry/backoff) and the v0.4.7 target have no UAT reports. process.mjs was deleted entirely, creating a latent import-breakage risk. This should not ship until the untested core modules are covered and a v0.4.6+ validation pass is documented.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs changes lack dedicated test coverage
findings.body: The core API client (scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs) has 155 lines changed across the diff, incorporating retry integration and BigModel error dispatch. There is no tests/glm-client.test.mjs or any test file whose name clearly targets it. glm-client.mjs is the single trust boundary to the upstream LLM API — it handles authentication tokens, request serialization, response parsing, and now retry/error-classification wiring. A silent misclassification or a malformed retry on a non-idempotent mutation call could corrupt job state without any test catching it. The existing tests/result-propagation.test.mjs (69 lines) is too small to exercise the full client surface.
findings.recommendation: Add a dedicated tests/glm-client.test.mjs that exercises every exported function, including retry-on-error-class scenarios, auth-header injection, and malformed-response handling. Block ship until coverage is confirmed.

findings.title: No UAT evidence for v0.4.6 or v0.4.7 release candidates
findings.body: UAT reports exist under test-automation/uat-reports/ for v0.4.4 and v0.4.5 only. The v0.4.6 release introduced BigModel error dispatch, auto retry/backoff, and retired the verify-release CI workflow. The v0.4.7 sampling-sweep target (this review) has no UAT directory at all. Two consecutive releases of runtime-critical features lack any end-to-end validation evidence. The only coverage is unit tests, which cannot catch integration-level failures like retry storms against the real API, background-mode process lifecycle issues, or state-file corruption under concurrent access.
findings.recommendation: Produce a test-automation/uat-reports/v0.4.6/ directory with at minimum: a run-uat.sh, scenario JSONs covering retry-on-server-error and background-mode lifecycle, and a report.md documenting pass/fail. Repeat for v0.4.7 before tagging.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs and job-control.mjs changes untested at unit level
findings.body: scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) and scripts/lib/job-control.mjs (20 lines changed) manage the background/wait execution mode introduced in v0.4.5. Neither has a correspondingly named test file. tests/job-render.test.mjs (144 lines) likely covers rendering, not job lifecycle persistence or cleanup. If background-mode job state is not correctly persisted or cleaned up on crash, users will encounter orphaned jobs that cannot be resumed or cancelled — a silent failure that is hard to detect in production.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs covering: job creation, state persistence, crash-recovery, concurrent access, and cleanup. Add tests/job-control.test.mjs for background/wait mode switching.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted — latent import-breakage risk
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) was entirely removed in this diff. If any remaining file still contains a bare `import … from './process.mjs'` or `from '../lib/process.mjs'`, the import will resolve to undefined at runtime. The retry.mjs module (203 new lines) may have absorbed process.mjs functionality, but without verifying every import site, there is a risk that a less-trafficked code path (e.g., error recovery or a hook) still references the deleted module and will crash only under specific conditions.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs' scripts/` across the codebase to confirm no remaining import references. If any exist, update them to the replacement module. Add a CI check (or extend check-imports.mjs) that verifies all import targets resolve to existing files.

findings.title: retry.mjs may retry non-idempotent operations
findings.body: scripts/lib/retry.mjs (203 new lines) implements retry/backoff logic. Without seeing the code, the concern is whether the retry layer distinguishes between idempotent GET/read operations and non-idempotent POST/write operations. If a job-submission or state-mutation call to BigModel is retried on a transient error, but the first attempt actually succeeded (slow response), the result is duplicated work or corrupted state. The 352-line test file suggests thorough unit coverage, but the integration with glm-client.mjs (which lacks its own test) means the retry-on-non-idempotent path is untested end-to-end.
findings.recommendation: Verify that retry.mjs accepts a configurable `idempotent` flag or that glm-client.mjs wraps mutation calls with retry disabled. Add an integration test that confirms a POST-like call is not retried even when the server returns a retryable error code.

next_steps: Run `grep -rn 'process\.mjs' scripts/` to confirm no dangling imports from the deleted module.
Create tests/glm-client.test.mjs with coverage for auth, retry integration, and error classification paths.
Produce test-automation/uat-reports/v0.4.6/ and v0.4.7/ with at minimum retry-on-error and background-mode scenarios before tagging v0.4.7.
Verify retry.mjs does not retry non-idempotent (mutation) API calls — add an idempotency guard or per-call opt-out.
Add tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs covering job lifecycle, crash recovery, and concurrent state access.
